Per-sample observations

Sample A (Image 1)
- Color and transparency: Golden/yellow; semi-transparent with mild diffuse haze.
- Clots/fibrin/structures: Yes—very faint cloudiness and soft flocculent areas near the lower half; no discrete firm clot.
- Coagulation stage: early_fibrin.
- Density and homogeneity: Mostly homogeneous liquid with a slightly denser, hazy basal region; a few minute specks.

Sample B (Image 2)
- Color and transparency: Golden/yellow; semi-transparent with visible turbidity.
- Clots/fibrin/structures: Yes—small flocculent aggregates and a thin filament/strand visible near the left side; liquid otherwise free-flowing. (The strand appears internal but exact position on/inside wall is uncertain.)
- Coagulation stage: early_fibrin.
- Density and homogeneity: Heterogeneous with localized cloudy spots and fine particulates.
- Unique features: Curled filamentous structure and a few discrete cloudlets; scattered wall droplets above liquid level (likely condensation, not part of sample).

Sample C (Image 3)
- Color and transparency: Golden/yellow; semi-transparent.
- Clots/fibrin/structures: Yes—broad, translucent gel-like sheet/adherent mass along the right wall with a hazy basal rim; liquid phase remains around it.
- Coagulation stage: partial_clot.
- Density and homogeneity: Markedly heterogeneous due to a coherent clot-like region plus surrounding liquid.
- Unique features: Wall-adherent lamellar/curtain-like structure occupying a substantial area of the lower-right; more organized than A or B.

Comparison
- Most advanced coagulation: Sample C shows the most organized, cohesive clot-like structure.
- Least advanced coagulation: Sample A appears the least progressed, with only subtle haze and minimal structure.
- Visible differences: All three share similar color, but the amount and organization of coagulation structures differ—A minimal/di diffuse, B with small flocs and a filament, C with a large adherent sheet.
- Overall visual difference: Moderate (color comparable; structure burden and organization clearly differ).

Sample A (Image 1)
1) Color/transparency: Central bright yellow; outer rim pale yellow–green. Center is semi-transparent; rim is near-opaque/opal.
2) Clots/fibrin/coagulation structures: Yes. The sample forms a cohesive, gel-like mass with stable edges and many trapped microbubbles; no discrete filamentous strands are resolved.
3) Coagulation stage: full_coagulation (appearance of a consolidated gel/clot).
4) Density/homogeneity: Visually high viscosity; non‑uniform (clearer center, more opaque periphery).
5) Unique features: Pancake‑like disk on the surface; fine speckling from trapped bubbles; halo effect at the boundary.

Sample B (Image 2)

2) Clots/fibrin/coagulation structures: No visible clots, strands, or aggregates.

openai.raw:
Per-sample assessments (independent)

Sample A (Image 1)
- Color and transparency: Pale yellow core with a faint, clearer peripheral halo; overall semi-transparent with a more opaque central mass.
- Clots/fibrin/coagulation structures: Yes. A single, compact gelled mass with sharp boundaries. No obvious free fibrin strands; surface shows a distinct flattened/indented “cap.”
- Coagulation stage: full_coagulation (appears firm, cohesive, and well-formed).
- Density and homogeneity: High apparent density; relatively homogeneous central body; minimal visible bubbles.
- Unique features: Hemispheric/cylindrical plug shape; crown-like indentation on the top; adherent thin clear film against the container wall.

Sample B (Image 2)
- Color and transparency: Pale yellow core within a larger, paler translucent matrix; overall semi-transparent, with the periphery more transparent than the core.
- Clots/fibrin/coagulation structures: Yes. Less compact mass; faint internal veils/striae suggestive of fibrin sheets; small surface bubbles; edges spread with a wet meniscus.
- Coagulation stage: partial_clot.
- Density and homogeneity: Lower apparent density than A; heterogeneous—denser core with looser outer zone.
- Unique features: Layered appearance with visible internal filaments; broader, flatter footprint.

Sample C (Image 3)
- Color and transparency: Pale yellow central cores with surrounding translucent, slightly gray-green–tinted gel; semi-transparent overall.
- Clots/fibrin/coagulation structures: Yes. Three discrete dome-shaped aggregates, each with a defined opaque core; no long external strands visible.
- Coagulation stage: partial_clot (appears more compact than B but less than A).
- Density and homogeneity: Intermediate density; relatively uniform domes with smooth glossy surfaces; few to no bubbles.
- Unique features: Consistent morphology across the three domes; crisp boundaries and pronounced central cores.

Comparison
- Most advanced coagulation: Sample A.
- Least advanced coagulation: Sample B.
- Visible differences: A is the most compact and opaque with a sharply defined plug; B is more spread out with visible internal striae and small bubbles; C forms smooth, consistent domes with intermediate compactness and minimal stranding.
- Overall visual difference between samples: Moderate.

Independent assessments

Sample A (Image 1)
- Color and transparency: Pale yellow; overall semi-transparent. Central droplet appears more opaque than surrounding fluid/film.
- Clots/fibrin/coagulation structures: Yes. A cohesive, lens-shaped central mass adherent to the wall with a thin, translucent rim; a few fine filamentous strands are faintly visible near the droplet edge.
- Coagulation stage: partial_clot (localized clot without extensive spread).
- Density and homogeneity: Dense central mass with a thinner peripheral film; clearly heterogeneous.
- Unique features: Smooth, well-defined droplet bordered by a pale halo; minimal lateral spread on the surface.

Sample B (Image 2)
- Color and transparency: Pale yellow center with a broad, grey‑yellow translucent film spreading outward; semi-transparent overall.
- Clots/fibrin/coagulation structures: Yes. Central gelatinous mound plus an extensive membrane-like sheet exhibiting polygonal cracking and fine radiating striae; numerous small circular inclusions (bubbles/particulates) embedded in the film.
- Coagulation stage: partial_clot (advanced), with widespread fibrin sheet formation but variable thickness and cracking.
- Density and homogeneity: Markedly heterogeneous—thin cracked regions alternating with denser zones around the center.
- Unique features: Large, reticulated “craquelure” pattern with radial lines and dotted inclusions across the sheet.

Sample C (Image 3)
- Color and transparency: More intensely yellow than A and B; semi-transparent film with an opaque central mound.
- Clots/fibrin/coagulation structures: Yes. Central clot surrounded by a thick, continuous annular film; pronounced reticular/honeycomb pattern with rounded compartments and dense microtexture toward the center.
- Coagulation stage: full_coagulation (most advanced appearance among the three).
- Density and homogeneity: Overall denser and more continuous film than B; still heterogeneous but fewer wide cracks.
- Unique features: Robust yellow annular band with “honeycomb/paver” compartments; denser microtextured zone immediately peripheral to the central clot.

Comparison
- Most advanced coagulation: Sample C.
- Least advanced coagulation: Sample A.
- Visible differences:
  - Extent of fibrin/membrane spread: minimal in A; broad but cracked in B; thick and continuous in C.
  - Color intensity: A < B ≈ C, with C most yellow.
  - Surface morphology: A shows a simple droplet and thin rim; B shows wide polygonal cracking with many inclusions; C shows a denser, more uniform annular honeycomb.
- Overall visual difference between samples: pronounced.
